Hardened Bone Spaulders  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 12
SV FIRE: +12 SV DISEASE: +12 SV COLD: +12 SV MAGIC: +12 SV POISON: +12
WT: 15.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL except HUM ERU HIE DEF HEF DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 21 2001 at 11:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) well this is one heck of a cool item for my halfling druid or any big race or little race character too. i'm just wondering though what is up with a piece that can be worn by big races or small races but not medium wearing races.... with the exception of the iksar and vah shir. Iksars can't wear plate, i know, and vah shir to my knowledge can. It's just a little surprising that they leave out all the elves and the humans(erudite,human,barbarian) out of the mix. My only idea is that this must be designed for the newest sever which has basis of roleplaying. Seems they giving the evils, and the shorties an advantage.
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 02 2001 at 2:11 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) imbued granite spauders are for the medium races, they decided to make one for us smalls, and the bigs.
Races & Classes
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2001 at 7:40 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591770191902
Scholar
92 posts
I don't know why this concept seems so hard to grasp or master, but it seems like every third item that is posted (here or on any other boards, for that matter...), there is some arguement like "since when can dwarves be bards?" or "wood elves can't be a shaman or paladin!" or "<insert your favorite can't be race/class combo here>"...

Here is the deal, these combinations show up because of the way they are coded ("programmed") by the designers when they create them. Rather than list each race and each class out in detail in the information for each item in the game, they have a code which indicates a particular 'group' of races or classes. They obviously have class group codes for things like, 'chain-wearing classes' and 'non-wisdom caster classes' and 'all melee classes', etc., etc. And they also have racial group codes for things such as 'small races' and 'evil races' and 'large races' etc., etc. When they design an item, they rate it equipable for a combination of codes, so you end up with things like gnome rangers or ogre monks looking like "possible" classes the item was meant for. As an example, I may want to design an item usable by Barbaian Rogues. I could use a code specifically for the rogue class, and then a code for large races. It would look like this:
Class: ROG
Race: BAR TRL OGR VAH
That does not mean that either trolls or ogres can suddenly be rogues, just that they happened to be included by the codes the designer used to specify the "usability data" for that item.

So when you see these items with 'impossible' race class combinations, just remember that they ended up there because of the codes the programmers used to specify usability data, not because Verant doesn't understand your Iksar cannot be a Cleric, or that they are suddenly going to allow Erudites to become a Shaman. No need to cry or whine or lament or disparage or argue or puzzle or moan or etc., etc., ... just deal with it and get over it.
▲ Page top

# Oct 20 2001 at 3:56 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Allow me to explain it to you, hopefully, in a way that you can understand:
"there is only one class on here which can't use them, that is ranger"
This person acknowledges that Rangers are listed as being able to use the item, but goes on to point out that none of the races listed can be Rangers, unless Verant is going to allow the Vah to become one.
So please, read the post before you make your ignorant comments.
Thank you.

#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 19 2001 at 7:43 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I go on raids almost daily i just picked one of these up 12 to all resist but low ac. although it has better ac than my pegaus feather cloak which i wear most of the time so i can just click lev and fly away. but when i go on raids now im gonna have to throw this on my shoulds esspecially when fighting dragons and those "GODS" that always seem to die faster than mortals. ill give the gods one thing They sure are tough and any one that says thay can solo them " there A DAMN LIAR".
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 18 2001 at 6:21 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) WHY do they have classes listed that can never come from the races listed on this thing..
RE: classes
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 12:04 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591754124041
133 posts
I believe the classes Uurrsonn is trying to point out are Bard and Ranger...we know that none of the other races besides the Vah'Shir can be these....so...this could lead to speculation...heh.
▲ Page top
Class Combos
QuoteReply
# Oct 22 2001 at 10:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757464810
156 posts
You could speculate on Vah Shir Rangers but you'd probably be wrong.

Its most likely the same situation that Verant has done before. Its an editing mistake. They make an item and default it to "All but INT Casters" and then edit the races and take out those that they don't want to use it.

What they forget to do is check the remaining Class/Race combos to take out any that don't still fit.

Vah Shir have been tentatively announced that they'll be able to be Warriors, Bards, Rogues, Shaman and Beastlords. While a Vah Shir Ranger would be cool, it's likely just a typo.
▲ Page top
